Description: This week your trainer walks you through AFF's flagship products and the reasons behind them. By the end of the week you'll understand exactly what you're offering to clients and why it matters.

Join the AFF Discord server and link your account so you get access to your phase training channels, weekly training reminders, and team resources. Use the button below to connect — it takes 10 seconds.

Schedule your pre-licensing course and exam with the licensing coordinator. They'll walk you through the process, help you pick a test date, and answer any questions along the way. Use the button below to reach them directly.
Sit down with your trainer for your own Personal Financial Review. This is both a real planning session for your finances and your first hands-on experience with the tool you'll use to help families.
A company-wide walkthrough of what to focus on in your first 30 days. Held Saturdays at 11am Eastern — your trainer will send you the link and details.
This week you'll build out your lead-generation marketing plan with your trainer. Come prepared with ideas about your natural market.
Work with your CFT to build your personal Business Marketing Plan. This becomes your 90-day playbook: target market, daily activity targets, and growth goals.

Pass your state life insurance exam. The moment you pass, schedule your post-licensing call with the licensing coordinator and your EMD — that's where your next steps get mapped out. Use the button below to reach out.
Schedule your fingerprinting and file your state license application. The licensing coordinator handles this with you — use the button below to send a request.
This is what gets you appointed to all of the different carriers. The licensing coordinator handles the submission for you — send a request directly from this item.
Complete your Continuing Education courses: AML, Annuity, and Ethics. The licensing coordinator will point you to the provider and help you past any snags.
Apply for your Errors & Omissions (E&O) insurance policy. The licensing coordinator walks you through the application.
Set up direct deposit so your commissions land in your account automatically. The licensing coordinator will help you finish this.
This week you'll start scheduling Field Training Appointments (FTAs) with your Certified Field Trainer.
Learn the scheduling and presentation scripts you'll use on your Field Training calls. Drill them until they feel natural.
Schedule your first 10 Field Training Appointments alongside your trainer. Once these are booked, you're ready to move into Phase 2.

Identify the first 3 people from your network you'd like to introduce to the AFF opportunity. Your trainer will guide you through the conversations — these become the foundation for your field training.
Your first Field Training Appointment — a live appointment with your CFT trainer. FTA 1 is typically with your immediate family (spouse, parents) to practice in a supportive environment.
Field Training Appointment 2 — continue building confidence running live appointments alongside your CFT trainer.
Field Training Appointment 3 — your presenter role should be growing. Aim to lead more of the appointment with your trainer observing.
Field Training Appointment 4.
Field Training Appointment 5 — halfway point. By now you should feel comfortable with the full AFF presentation flow.
Field Training Appointment 6.
Field Training Appointment 7.
Field Training Appointment 8.
Field Training Appointment 9.
Field Training Appointment 10 — completing all 10 FTAs demonstrates you are ready to run appointments independently and begin building your team.
Officially promoted to Associate level within AFF. This recognizes your completion of Phase 1 requirements and confirms you're ready for independent field work.
Sponsor and onboard your first direct agent — someone you personally recruited who has joined AFF. Building your team starts here.
Sponsor and onboard your second direct agent on your team.
Sponsor and onboard your third direct agent. Three actives is the foundation of a growing agency.
Help your first client — complete a full financial needs analysis and present a real solution. Document the case in your Policies tab.
Help your second client. Each client you serve builds your experience and your track record.
Help your third client. Three cases demonstrates consistent client service — a major milestone.
Receive and activate your physical or digital life insurance license from your state. You are now fully authorized to sell and earn commissions.
Earn your first $1,000 in commission from issued and paid policies. This milestone proves the system works — and it's just the start of what's possible.
Attend CFT (Certified Field Trainer) In Progress sessions hosted by AFF leadership. These are the advanced classes that teach you how to train and develop new agents.
Your CFT trainer officially signs off that you can run appointments independently and are ready to begin training others. This is a formal milestone in your development.
The CFT Coordinator — a senior AFF trainer — reviews your performance and approves your readiness for CFT designation.
The Elite Marketing Director reviews and signs off on your CFT designation. This is the final approval before you advance to Phase 4.
Run a complete first client appointment independently — conducting the financial needs analysis and presenting the AFF system without your trainer present.
Run the follow-up (second) appointment independently — presenting personalized solutions and closing the case on your own.
Master all phone scripts: client scheduling calls, recruiting calls, and follow-up calls. Demonstrate the ability to set quality appointments via phone alone.
Conduct a full recruiting interview with a prospective agent candidate using the official AFF interview process. Document it in your Business Partners tab.
Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of the full AFF system: the business model, compensation structure, agent career path, and how to explain it to prospects.
Develop working knowledge of the top 5 AFF products. You must be able to identify which product fits each client scenario and explain the core benefits clearly.
Complete product certification on Fixed Indexed Annuities (FIA) — tax-deferred retirement vehicles with upside tied to a market index and downside protection.
Complete training on Final Expense products — simplified-issue permanent life insurance designed to cover end-of-life costs. A key product for the senior market.
Complete product training on Term Life and Term with Living Benefits. Understand when to recommend pure term vs. term with critical illness or chronic illness riders.
Master the Indexed Universal Life (IUL) / Family Bank strategy — AFF's signature wealth-building product. Clients use it to build tax-free retirement income and a family legacy.
Complete training on the Million Dollar Baby strategy — an IUL funded for infants or children that grows into a tax-advantaged wealth vehicle over their lifetime.
Accumulate 45,000 production points from issued and paid policies. This is the primary production threshold for Marketing Director (MD) qualification.
Maintain at least $1,250 in monthly premium production during Month 1. Consistent monthly premium demonstrates sustainable client activity.
Maintain at least $1,250 in monthly premium production during Month 2.
Maintain at least $1,250 in monthly premium production during Month 3. Three consecutive months proves you have a repeatable, sustainable business.
Net License 1 — an active, licensed agent on your team who you directly or indirectly sponsored. Net means they are currently active and producing.
Net License 2 — a second active licensed agent on your team.
Net License 3 — a third active licensed agent on your team.
Net License 4 — a fourth active licensed agent on your team.
Net License 5 — five active licensed agents on your team. This is the team size required to qualify for Marketing Director.
Maintain 150,000 net production points over any consecutive 6-month period. This demonstrates that you are leading a high-performing team, not just an active producer.
Develop and promote at least one agent from your team to Marketing Director level. Leadership multiplication — not just your own production — defines the EMD path.
Net License 1 of 20 required for EMD — an active licensed agent in your organization.
Net License 2 of 20.
Net License 3 of 20.
Net License 4 of 20.
Net License 5 of 20.
Net License 6 of 20.
Net License 7 of 20.
Net License 8 of 20.
Net License 9 of 20.
Net License 10 of 20 — halfway to EMD team size.
Net License 11 of 20.
Net License 12 of 20.
Net License 13 of 20.
Net License 14 of 20.
Net License 15 of 20.
Net License 16 of 20.
Net License 17 of 20.
Net License 18 of 20.
Net License 19 of 20.
Net License 20 of 20 — the final team size requirement for Elite Marketing Director. You have built a true agency.
